mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, which forces equity market repricing and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which generates positive short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day return direction with an accuracy rate exceeding 55 percent during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above the three percent threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above three percent coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in more than sixty percent of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over any twenty-four-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects aggregate economic expansion, which directly correlates with corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day return direction, with a rank correlation coefficient below 0.01 across all available data.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding one percent signals acute supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, which triggers immediate equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than one percent fail to precede negative 5-day returns in at least seventy percent of historical occurrences.
